---
title: "Updated dataset_30 Mar 2026"
author: "Irena Austin"
date: "2026-04-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}

# ============================================================
# Step 1. Setup: Load Packages & Data
# ============================================================
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, janitor, lubridate, skimr, caret, glmnet, 
  broom, ggplot2, knitr, kableExtra, arrow, corrplot,
  rpart, rpart.plot, randomForest, ranger
)

# Load the full 186-column Parquet file
flights_raw <- open_dataset("C:/Users/ZyrIr/OneDrive/Desktop/flights_canonical_2019.parquet") %>%
  collect() %>%
  clean_names()

# ============================================================
# Step 2. Master Cleaning & Feature Engineering Pipeline
# ============================================================
# This creates a single 'source of truth' for all subsequent analysis
df_final <- flights_raw %>%
  # 1. Filter for your target NYC hubs
  filter(origin == "BWI", dest %in% c("JFK", "EWR")) %>%
  
  # 2. SELECT: Include every column needed for Models, Hubs, and Holidays
  select(
    flight_date, reporting_airline, tail_number, arr_delay, dep_delay, 
    carrier_delay, weather_delay, taxi_out, dep_hour_local, 
    day_of_week, month, dep_temp_c, dep_wind_speed_m_s, 
    dep_ceiling_height_m, prev_arr_delay, turnaround_minutes, 
    aircraft_leg_number_day, crs_elapsed_time, actual_elapsed_time, distance
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. CLEAN: Handle NAs (The fix for na.fail errors)
  mutate(
    across(c(prev_arr_delay, weather_delay, carrier_delay), ~replace_na(., 0)),
    month_day = format(as.Date(flight_date), "%m-%d")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(arr_delay), !is.na(dep_delay)) %>%
  
  # 4. FEATURE ENGINEERING: Strategy, Holidays, and Plane Size Proxy
  mutate(
    is_delayed        = as.factor(ifelse(arr_delay >= 15, "Yes", "No")),
    is_weekend        = as.factor(ifelse(day_of_week %in% c(6, 7), "Yes", "No")),
    is_rush_hour      = as.factor(ifelse(dep_hour_local %in% c(7, 8, 16, 17, 18), "Yes", "No")),
    reporting_airline = as.factor(reporting_airline),
    buffer            = crs_elapsed_time - actual_elapsed_time,
    is_holiday        = as.factor(case_when(
      month_day >= "11-22" & month_day <= "12-02" ~ "Holiday", # Thanksgiving
      month_day >= "12-19" | month_day <= "01-05" ~ "Holiday", # Winter
      TRUE ~ "Non-Holiday"
    )),
    # PLANE SIZE PROXY: Feature Aggregation/Heuristic Classification
    plane_size_score = case_when(
      distance > 1500 ~ "Large",
      distance > 600  ~ "Medium",
      TRUE            ~ "Small"
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# Step 3. Descriptive Visuals & Business Intelligence
# ============================================================

# 1. Correlation Plot (Numeric only)
df_final %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% drop_na() %>% 
  cor() %>% corrplot(method = "color", addCoef.col = "black", tl.cex = 0.7, title="Predictor Matrix")

# 2. Scheduling Honesty (Lollipop Chart)
all_airline_buffers <- flights_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(crs_elapsed_time), !is.na(actual_elapsed_time)) %>%
  mutate(buffer_mins = crs_elapsed_time - actual_elapsed_time) %>%
  group_by(reporting_airline) %>%
  summarise(avg_buffer = mean(buffer_mins, na.rm = TRUE), count = n()) %>%
  filter(count > 100)

ggplot(all_airline_buffers, aes(x = reorder(reporting_airline, avg_buffer), y = avg_buffer)) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = reporting_airline, yend = 0), color = "grey") +
  geom_point(size = 4, aes(color = avg_buffer)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightpink", high = "purple") + coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Airline Strategy: The Buffer Rankings", y="Avg Buffer (min)")

# ============================================================
# Step 4. Tree-Based Machine Learning (NO NA ERRORS)
# ============================================================

# Prepare the cleaned-subset for modeling
df_model <- df_final %>%
  select(is_delayed, dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s, 
         aircraft_leg_number_day, plane_size_score, is_holiday, is_weekend) %>%
  drop_na() # <--- CRITICAL FIX: Removes any remaining missing values

# 1. Decision Tree (The 'Logic Map')
tree_fit <- rpart(is_delayed ~ ., data = df_model, method = "class")
rpart.plot(tree_fit, extra = 101, main = "Decision Tree: BWI-NYC Delay Logic")

# 2. Random Forest (The 'Powerhouse')
rf_fit <- randomForest(is_delayed ~ ., data = df_model, ntree = 100, importance = TRUE)
varImpPlot(rf_fit, main = "Variable Importance: What truly causes NYC delays?")

# 3. National Scale Model (Ranger)
df_full_national <- flights_raw %>%
  select(arr_delay, dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s, aircraft_leg_number_day, distance) %>%
  mutate(is_delayed = as.factor(ifelse(arr_delay >= 15, "Yes", "No"))) %>%
  drop_na()

full_rf_ranger <- ranger(is_delayed ~ ., data = df_full_national, 
                         importance = "impurity", num.trees = 100,
                         num.threads = parallel::detectCores() - 1)

print(full_rf_ranger)


```
```{r cars}
# ============================================================
# OR568 | Flight Delay Analysis — BWI–NYC Hub
# Enhanced: PCA + Nonlinear Modeling
# ============================================================

# ============================================================
# Step 1. Setup: Load Packages & Data
# ============================================================
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, janitor, lubridate, skimr, caret, glmnet,
  broom, ggplot2, knitr, kableExtra, arrow, corrplot,
  rpart, rpart.plot, randomForest, ranger,
  e1071,        # SVM (nonlinear kernel)
  nnet,         # Neural net (nonlinear)
  kernlab,      # ksvm with RBF kernel
  factoextra,   # PCA biplots & scree plots
  FactoMineR    # PCA engine
)

# Load the full Parquet file
flights_raw <- open_dataset("C:/Users/ZyrIr/OneDrive/Desktop/flights_canonical_2019.parquet") %>%
  collect() %>%
  clean_names()

# ============================================================
# Step 2. Master Cleaning & Feature Engineering Pipeline
# ============================================================
df_final <- flights_raw %>%
  filter(origin == "BWI", dest %in% c("JFK", "EWR")) %>%
  select(
    flight_date, reporting_airline, tail_number, arr_delay, dep_delay,
    carrier_delay, weather_delay, taxi_out, dep_hour_local,
    day_of_week, month, dep_temp_c, dep_wind_speed_m_s,
    dep_ceiling_height_m, prev_arr_delay, turnaround_minutes,
    aircraft_leg_number_day, crs_elapsed_time, actual_elapsed_time, distance
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(prev_arr_delay, weather_delay, carrier_delay), ~replace_na(., 0)),
    month_day = format(as.Date(flight_date), "%m-%d")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(arr_delay), !is.na(dep_delay)) %>%
  mutate(
    is_delayed        = as.factor(ifelse(arr_delay >= 15, "Yes", "No")),
    is_weekend        = as.factor(ifelse(day_of_week %in% c(6, 7), "Yes", "No")),
    is_rush_hour      = as.factor(ifelse(dep_hour_local %in% c(7, 8, 16, 17, 18), "Yes", "No")),
    reporting_airline = as.factor(reporting_airline),
    buffer            = crs_elapsed_time - actual_elapsed_time,
    is_holiday        = as.factor(case_when(
      month_day >= "11-22" & month_day <= "12-02" ~ "Holiday",
      month_day >= "12-19" | month_day <= "01-05" ~ "Holiday",
      TRUE ~ "Non-Holiday"
    )),
    plane_size_score  = case_when(
      distance > 1500 ~ "Large",
      distance > 600  ~ "Medium",
      TRUE            ~ "Small"
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# Step 3. Descriptive Visuals & Business Intelligence
# ============================================================

# Correlation Plot (Numeric only)
df_final %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% drop_na() %>%
  cor() %>% corrplot(method = "color", addCoef.col = "black",
                     tl.cex = 0.7, title = "Predictor Correlation Matrix")

# Scheduling Honesty (Lollipop Chart — national scope)
all_airline_buffers <- flights_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(crs_elapsed_time), !is.na(actual_elapsed_time)) %>%
  mutate(buffer_mins = crs_elapsed_time - actual_elapsed_time) %>%
  group_by(reporting_airline) %>%
  summarise(avg_buffer = mean(buffer_mins, na.rm = TRUE), count = n()) %>%
  filter(count > 100)

ggplot(all_airline_buffers, aes(x = reorder(reporting_airline, avg_buffer), y = avg_buffer)) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = reporting_airline, yend = 0), color = "grey") +
  geom_point(size = 4, aes(color = avg_buffer)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightpink", high = "purple") + coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "Airline Strategy: The Buffer Rankings", y = "Avg Buffer (min)")

# ============================================================
# Step 4. PCA — Dimensionality Reduction on Numeric Predictors
# ============================================================
# Select only numeric predictors relevant to delay
df_pca_input <- df_final %>%
  select(
    dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s, dep_temp_c,
    dep_ceiling_height_m, prev_arr_delay, turnaround_minutes,
    aircraft_leg_number_day, crs_elapsed_time, actual_elapsed_time,
    buffer, distance, taxi_out, weather_delay, carrier_delay
  ) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  scale() %>%                   # z-score standardize: REQUIRED before PCA
  as.data.frame()

# Run PCA
pca_result <- PCA(df_pca_input, graph = FALSE)

# 4a. Scree Plot — How many PCs explain the variance?
fviz_eig(pca_result, addlabels = TRUE, barfill = "#7B68EE", barcolor = "#7B68EE") +
  labs(title = "PCA Scree Plot: Variance Explained per Component",
       x = "Principal Component", y = "% Variance Explained") +
  theme_minimal()

# 4b. Biplot — Variable loadings on PC1 & PC2
fviz_pca_var(pca_result,
             col.var    = "contrib",
             gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
             repel      = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "PCA Biplot: Variable Contributions to PC1 & PC2")

# 4c. Extract PC scores (retain enough PCs to explain ~80% variance)
n_pcs <- which(cumsum(pca_result$eig[, "percentage of variance"]) >= 80)[1]
cat("PCs needed to explain 80% variance:", n_pcs, "\n")

pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pca_result$ind$coord[, 1:n_pcs])
colnames(pca_scores) <- paste0("PC", 1:n_pcs)

# Re-attach target label (rows must align — use same drop_na logic)
df_pca_labeled <- df_final %>%
  select(
    is_delayed,
    dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s, dep_temp_c,
    dep_ceiling_height_m, prev_arr_delay, turnaround_minutes,
    aircraft_leg_number_day, crs_elapsed_time, actual_elapsed_time,
    buffer, distance, taxi_out, weather_delay, carrier_delay
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

# Safety check: row counts must match before binding
stopifnot(nrow(df_pca_labeled) == nrow(pca_scores))
df_pca_model <- bind_cols(select(df_pca_labeled, is_delayed), pca_scores)

# ============================================================
# Step 5. Train/Test Split (shared across all models)
# ============================================================
set.seed(42)

# Split A: Original feature set (for tree-based models)
df_model <- df_final %>%
  select(is_delayed, dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s,
         aircraft_leg_number_day, plane_size_score, is_holiday, is_weekend) %>%
  drop_na()

train_idx  <- createDataPartition(df_model$is_delayed, p = 0.75, list = FALSE)
train_orig <- df_model[train_idx, ]
test_orig  <- df_model[-train_idx, ]

# Split B: PCA feature set (for nonlinear models)
train_pca_idx <- createDataPartition(df_pca_model$is_delayed, p = 0.75, list = FALSE)
train_pca     <- df_pca_model[train_pca_idx, ]
test_pca      <- df_pca_model[-train_pca_idx, ]

# ============================================================
# Step 6. Tree-Based Models (Original Features)
# ============================================================

# 6a. Decision Tree
tree_fit <- rpart(is_delayed ~ ., data = train_orig, method = "class",
                  control = rpart.control(cp = 0.01, maxdepth = 6))
rpart.plot(tree_fit, extra = 101, main = "Decision Tree: BWI–NYC Delay Logic")

tree_preds <- predict(tree_fit, test_orig, type = "class")
cat("\n--- Decision Tree Confusion Matrix ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(tree_preds, test_orig$is_delayed))

# 6b. Random Forest
rf_fit <- randomForest(is_delayed ~ ., data = train_orig,
                       ntree = 200, importance = TRUE)
varImpPlot(rf_fit, main = "Variable Importance: What Drives BWI–NYC Delays?")

rf_preds <- predict(rf_fit, test_orig)
cat("\n--- Random Forest Confusion Matrix ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(rf_preds, test_orig$is_delayed))

# 6c. Ranger (National Scale)
df_full_national <- flights_raw %>%
  select(arr_delay, dep_delay, dep_hour_local, dep_wind_speed_m_s,
         aircraft_leg_number_day, distance) %>%
  mutate(is_delayed = as.factor(ifelse(arr_delay >= 15, "Yes", "No"))) %>%
  drop_na()

full_rf_ranger <- ranger(
  is_delayed ~ ., data = df_full_national,
  importance  = "impurity", num.trees = 100,
  num.threads = parallel::detectCores() - 1
)
print(full_rf_ranger)

# ============================================================
# Step 7. Nonlinear Models on PCA Components
# ============================================================
# Using PCA scores removes multicollinearity — ideal for kernel methods

# 7a. SVM with Radial Basis Function (RBF) Kernel
# RBF maps inputs into a high-dimensional space to find nonlinear boundaries
cat("\nFitting SVM (RBF kernel) on PCA components...\n")
svm_fit <- ksvm(
  is_delayed ~ ., data = train_pca,
  kernel = "rbfdot",     # Radial Basis Function — captures nonlinear structure
  C      = 1,            # Regularization: higher = tighter fit
  kpar   = "automatic",  # Auto-tune gamma via median heuristic
  prob.model = TRUE
)

svm_preds <- predict(svm_fit, test_pca)
cat("\n--- SVM (RBF) Confusion Matrix ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(svm_preds, test_pca$is_delayed))

# 7b. Neural Network (nnet — single hidden layer)
# Nonlinear activation functions capture complex relationships between PCs
cat("\nFitting Neural Network on PCA components...\n")
nnet_fit <- nnet(
  is_delayed ~ ., data = train_pca,
  size    = 8,      # 8 hidden neurons
  decay   = 0.01,   # L2 weight decay (regularization)
  maxit   = 300,    # Max training iterations
  trace   = FALSE
)

nnet_preds <- predict(nnet_fit, test_pca, type = "class") %>% as.factor()
cat("\n--- Neural Network Confusion Matrix ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(nnet_preds, test_pca$is_delayed))

# 7c. Polynomial SVM (degree-3) — an alternative nonlinear kernel
cat("\nFitting SVM (Polynomial kernel, degree=3) on PCA components...\n")
svm_poly_fit <- ksvm(
  is_delayed ~ ., data = train_pca,
  kernel = "polydot",
  kpar   = list(degree = 3, scale = 1, offset = 1),
  C      = 1
)

svm_poly_preds <- predict(svm_poly_fit, test_pca)
cat("\n--- SVM (Polynomial) Confusion Matrix ---\n")
print(confusionMatrix(svm_poly_preds, test_pca$is_delayed))

# ============================================================
# Step 8. Model Comparison Summary Table
# ============================================================
# Helper to extract accuracy + kappa from a confusionMatrix object
extract_metrics <- function(cm, model_name, feature_set) {
  tibble(
    Model       = model_name,
    Features    = feature_set,
    Accuracy    = round(cm$overall["Accuracy"], 4),
    Kappa       = round(cm$overall["Kappa"], 4),
    Sensitivity = round(cm$byClass["Sensitivity"], 4),
    Specificity = round(cm$byClass["Specificity"], 4)
  )
}

cm_tree     <- confusionMatrix(tree_preds,     test_orig$is_delayed)
cm_rf       <- confusionMatrix(rf_preds,       test_orig$is_delayed)
cm_svm      <- confusionMatrix(svm_preds,      test_pca$is_delayed)
cm_nnet     <- confusionMatrix(nnet_preds,     test_pca$is_delayed)
cm_svm_poly <- confusionMatrix(svm_poly_preds, test_pca$is_delayed)

model_comparison <- bind_rows(
  extract_metrics(cm_tree,     "Decision Tree",         "Original"),
  extract_metrics(cm_rf,       "Random Forest",         "Original"),
  extract_metrics(cm_svm,      "SVM (RBF)",             "PCA"),
  extract_metrics(cm_nnet,     "Neural Network",        "PCA"),
  extract_metrics(cm_svm_poly, "SVM (Polynomial d=3)",  "PCA")
)

cat("\n========== MODEL COMPARISON ==========\n")
print(model_comparison)

# Visual comparison
ggplot(model_comparison, aes(x = reorder(Model, Accuracy), y = Accuracy, fill = Features)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(Accuracy, accuracy = 0.1)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip(ylim = c(0.5, 1.0)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Original" = "#7B68EE", "PCA" = "#20B2AA")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(title   = "Model Accuracy Comparison: Tree-Based vs. Nonlinear (PCA)",
       x = NULL, y = "Test Set Accuracy",
       caption = "PCA models trained on retained principal components (≥80% variance)")

# ============================================================
# Step 9. PCA Insight — Delay Group Separation on PC1 vs PC2
# ============================================================
# Do delayed/non-delayed flights cluster differently in PC space?
df_pca_viz <- df_pca_labeled %>%
  select(is_delayed) %>%
  bind_cols(as.data.frame(pca_result$ind$coord[, 1:2]) %>%
              rename(PC1 = Dim.1, PC2 = Dim.2))

ggplot(df_pca_viz, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = is_delayed)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Yes" = "#E63946", "No" = "#457B9D"),
                     labels = c("Yes" = "Delayed ≥15min", "No" = "On Time")) +
  stat_ellipse(level = 0.90, linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(title   = "PCA Space: Do Delayed Flights Cluster Separately?",
       subtitle = "90% confidence ellipses by delay class",
       color   = "Delayed?",
       caption = "BWI–NYC flights, 2019 BTS data")

ggplot(df_final, aes(x = is_holiday, y = arr_delay, fill = is_holiday)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-20, 100)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Holiday" = "firebrick", "Non-Holiday" = "darkgrey")) +
  labs(title = "The Holiday Tax: BWI-NYC Arrival Delays",
       subtitle = "Comparing FAA-defined delays during peak travel windows",
       x = "Season", y = "Arrival Delay (min)") +
  theme_minimal()


```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
